title: dombeya marivorahonensis
intro: Dombeya marivorahonensis, also known as the Madagascar wild pear, is a plant species in the family Sterculiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues. The roots, leaves, and bark of the Madagascar wild pear have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions and decoctions for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
